summary: ICP/Motoko app with a Communities tab featuring full community page layout: customizable top banner (image upload, colors, fonts), stats bar, draggable/reorderable horizontal tabs (Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules), functional post creation on Home tab, host-only edit controls with per-member permission toggling, Internet Identity authentication, on-chain binary image storage, and communities accessible after leaving the Communities tab. Nothing outside the Communities tab is modified.
architectural_notes: Backend: Motoko canister on Internet Computer; community data (members, posts, tab order, permissions, banners) stored on-chain
Images stored as binary data directly on-chain in the canister
No changes permitted outside the Communities tab

existing_features.title: The 'Open' button on the CommunityCard component (located at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/section[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/button[1]) must navigate to a full CommunityPage view when clicked. This navigation must work and persist even after the user has left the Communities tab and returned — the community should remain accessible by storing the active community ID in component state or context, not just URL params.

Create a CommunityPage component rendered inside the Communities tab. It must include: (1) a full-width top banner section displaying the community's banner image (or a solid color fallback), community name, and a host-only 'Edit Banner' control; (2) a stats bar below the banner showing member count and total post count; (3) a horizontal tab bar (Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules) rendered below the stats bar.

Implement host-only banner customization from within the CommunityPage. The 'Edit Banner' control (host-only) opens an inline editor or modal allowing the host to: upload a new banner image (stored on-chain as binary), pick a banner background color, select a font for the community title, and pick an accent color. Changes are saved via the updateBannerSettings backend method and immediately reflected on the page.

Implement draggable/reorderable horizontal tabs on the CommunityPage. By default, only the community host can reorder tabs by dragging them. The host can toggle per-member tab-reorder permission (individually, per principal) from within the community page. Non-permitted members see the tabs as fixed and non-draggable. Tab order is persisted on-chain via updateTabOrder and loaded via getTabOrder on page mount.

Implement the Home tab content on the CommunityPage. It must include a post creation form (Internet Identity authentication required) with a text input and optional image upload (stored on-chain as binary via createPost). Below the form, render a chronological feed of all posts for the community, each showing author display name, post text, optional image, and timestamp. Fetched via getPosts.

Implement the Chat tab content on the CommunityPage as a non-functional UI mockup placeholder. Render a greyed-out chat layout that visually resembles a messaging interface (input bar, message bubbles) but all interactive elements are disabled or visually muted. Display a 'Coming Soon' or 'Private messaging — coming soon' label overlay.

Implement placeholder layouts for the Lore, Polls, Quizzes, and Rules tabs on the CommunityPage. Each tab should render a styled placeholder card or section with the tab name as a heading and a 'Coming Soon' or informational message. These placeholders must visually match the community's theme (accent color, font).

Ensure communities remain accessible after the user navigates away from and returns to the Communities tab. The active community ID (if a community page was open) must be preserved in React state or context so the user is returned to the same community page view when they navigate back to the Communities tab.

Ensure all changes are strictly scoped to the Communities tab and its sub-components (CommunityCard, CommunitiesHubPage, and new CommunityPage). No files, components, hooks, or backend methods outside the Communities tab feature area should be modified.

new_feature_requests.summary: Open button navigates to full community page
Community page: customizable top banner (image upload, colors, fonts) editable by host
Community page: stats bar (members, posts, etc.)
Draggable/reorderable horizontal tabs (Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules) — host-only by default with per-member permission toggling
Chat tab: non-functional UI mockup (greyed-out chat layout) as placeholder
Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules tabs: placeholder layouts
Internet Identity authentication required to create communities and posts
On-chain binary image storage for community banners and post images
Communities remain accessible after leaving the Communities tab

Add backend data types and storage for community pages: CommunityPost (id, communityId, authorPrincipal, content, imageBlob, createdAt), CommunityTabOrder (communityId, tabOrder as array of tab names), CommunityTabPermissions (communityId, membersWithReorderPermission as array of principals), and extend the existing Community type to include bannerBlob (binary), bannerColor, bannerFont, accentColor. Provide canister methods: createPost(communityId, content, imageBlob?) -> PostId; getPosts(communityId) -> [CommunityPost]; updateBannerSettings(communityId, bannerBlob?, bannerColor?, bannerFont?, accentColor?) — host only; updateTabOrder(communityId, newOrder) — host or permitted member only; grantTabReorderPermission(communityId, principal) — host only; revokeTabReorderPermission(communityId, principal) — host only; getTabPermissions(communityId) -> [Principal]; getTabOrder(communityId) -> [Text].
